mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth and expanding retail sales consistently boost corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 6.5%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative or flat in >50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and stable consumer prices indicate healthy supply-side capacity expansion without inflationary pressure, compressing risk premiums and lifting equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are uncorrelated with the joint INDPRO/CPIAUCSL spread, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, rising policy rates combined with persistent inflation compress equity valuations by increasing the discount rate and eroding real cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in >60% of periods where FEDFUNDS rises consecutively for three months alongside CPIAUCSL above 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts reflects improving business confidence and consumer wealth, providing a steady upward bias to equity markets over the following week.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between HOUST month-over-month changes and SPX 5d returns falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales signals weakening aggregate demand, which reduces forward earnings estimates and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain positive or neutral in >55% of instances following a >2% monthly drop in RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market recovery, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to explain >5% of forward return variance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial output growth, manufacturing sector profitability expands, lifting industrial and broader equity indices over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show no significant difference between high INDPRO growth months and low INDPRO growth months (t-test p > 0.10).
